source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/03_제품별 인증서 & 성적서/2. 제품별 성적서/05_Blue/2. EMC/[CR]G1417CW_RF Test report_최종/[CR]G1417CW_RF Test report_300.328 V2.2.2.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: KES CE test report KES-RF240887 records PASS results for EN 300 328 V2.2.2 RF testing of the G1417CW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector and listed variants.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as traceable evidence of EN 300 328 V2.2.2 transmitter and receiver spurious-emissions testing for the listed detector models.
keyPoints: Client and manufacturer are H&abyz Co., Ltd.
Product item is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, model G1417CW, with seven listed variant model names.
Test method used is EN 300 328 V2.2.2 and the overall test result is PASS.
Transmitter unwanted emissions in the spurious domain and receiver spurious emissions are marked Pass.
Several EN 300 328 tests were not tested because the equipment is equipped with a certified module and the report refers to module report No. ER740701-11AC.

evidence.claim: The report number is KES-RF240887.
The document is identified as a CE test report.
The client is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
The tested product item is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product item : Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The primary model name is G1417CW.
The listed variant model names are G1417CWP, G1417FW, G1417FWP, G1717CW, G1717CWP, G1717FW, and G1717FWP.
evidence.evidenceText: Variant model name : G1417CWP, G1417FW, G1417FWP, G1717CW, G1717CWP, G1717FW, G1717FWP

evidence.claim: Testing was performed from 2025.01.01 to 2025.01.06.
The test method used was EN 300 328 V2.2.2.
The report gives an overall PASS test result.
The report date shown with the affirmation is 2025.01.20.
The revision history lists KES-RF240887 as Initial on 2025.01.20.
The EUT supports 2.4 GHz 802.11b/g/n_HT20 from 2412 MHz to 2472 MHz and 802.11n_HT40 from 2422 MHz to 2462 MHz.
The EUT supports 5 GHz UNII-1, UNII-2A, UNII-2C, and UNII-3 frequency ranges.
The modulation techniques are DSSS and OFDM.
The antenna specification lists PCB antennas with 2.79 dBi peak gain in the 2.4 GHz band and 3.38 dBi peak gain in the 5 GHz band.
evidence.evidenceText: ANT1 : PCB Antenna // Peak gain: 2.4 ㎓ band : 2.79 ㏈i / 5 ㎓ band : 3.38 ㏈i; ANT2 : PCB Antenna // Peak gain: 2.4 ㎓ band : 2.79 ㏈i / 5 ㎓ band : 3.38 ㏈i

evidence.claim: The transmitter unwanted emissions in the spurious domain test passed.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 300 328 4.3.2.9 Transmitter unwanted emissions in the spurious domain Pass
evidence.pageTarget: Transmitter unwanted emissions in the spurious domain

evidence.claim: The receiver spurious emissions test passed.

RF Output Power, Power Spectral Density, Occupied Channel Bandwidth, transmitter unwanted emissions in the out-of-band domain, and Receiver Blocking were not tested with a note to the certified module report.
evidence.evidenceText: RF Output Power N/T Note.1; Power Spectral Density N/T Note.1; Occupied Channel Bandwidth N/T Note.1; Transmitter unwanted emissions in the out-of-band domain N/T Note.1; Receiver Blocking N/T Note.1; Note.1 It is equipped with an certified module and please refer to the module report for test results. Module report No. : ER740701-11AC

evidence.claim: The report was tested only for MIMO at the applicant's request, while SISO is also supported.
evidence.evidenceText: It is also supports SISO, but This report was tested only for MIMO at the request of the applicant.

evidence.claim: The test mode described for spurious emissions was 802.11n_HT40.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: the document is a CE test report and states KOLAS accreditation, but no regulatory authority certificate or notified-body decision is provided.
Source metadata productSignal is Unclassified even though the text identifies the product item and model names.
Some application-form checkbox selections are unclear in the extracted text, so adaptive/non-adaptive and geo-location selections should be reviewed against the original PDF.
